info.title: AI-Kaeyris
info.description: Plateforme AI personnelle — monolithe modulaire FastAPI.

get.summary: Get Setup Status
Get Users

post.summary: Upload the audio of a session and enqueue its transcription.

delete.summary: Purge a session's audio file and reset the session so a new upload can be sent.
delete.description:
Drop the audio file on disk and reset the session to ``created``.

Use cases (matches data-model.md §3 retry arrow
``transcription_failed → audio_uploaded`` and the analogous
cancel-before-transcription path):

- The MJ uploaded the wrong file and wants to re-upload before the
  worker picks it up.
- Transcription failed and the MJ wants to try again with a different
  audio (or after the upstream provider has recovered).

Refused (409) when ``state ∈ {transcribing, transcribed}``: see
``logic.purge_audio_for_session`` for the rationale.

post.summary: (stub — Jalon 5) Open a live ingestion session.
post.description:
Always raises 501 ``live-not-implemented``.

The Pydantic body is validated first so a malformed payload still
returns 422, keeping the published contract honest even when the
implementation lands later.

get.summary: List the MJ's sessions.

post.description:
Create a session owned by the authenticated MJ.

The body holds the human title and the date the session actually
took place (``recorded_at``). State starts at ``created``; the
audio is uploaded separately via ``POST /sessions/{id}/audio``.
The optional ``campaign_context`` is a steering block for the LLM
(PNJ récurrents, ton, fil narratif) — see PATCH for updating it.

get.summary: Fetch one of the MJ's sessions.

patch.summary: Partially update a session (title and/or campaign_context).
patch.description:
Apply a partial update.

Only the fields present in the body are touched. To clear
``campaign_context``, send ``{"campaign_context": null}`` explicitly —
omitting the key leaves the existing value alone. Updating
``campaign_context`` does NOT re-run any previously generated
artefact; the new value affects only future generations.

``transcription_mode`` is immutable after creation (FR-002 of
feature 002): any PATCH body referencing it is rejected with 422.

get.summary: Chunked transcription of a non_diarised session (ordered).
get.description:
Return the chunks (text, ordre) of a non_diarised session.

Reserved for non_diarised sessions — diarised sessions use the
`GET /transcription` endpoint instead (409 wrong-mode otherwise).
Returns 404 transcription-not-ready if no chunks have been produced
yet for the session.

get.summary: List the current MJ's PJs.

post.summary: Create a PJ (player-character) owned by the current MJ.
post.description:
A PJ is stable across sessions — it's the narrative anchor that
later gets mapped to a diarisation ``speaker_label`` per session
(sub-lot 5b) and to a player API key (US4).

Duplicate name within the same MJ -> 409 ``duplicate-pj``.

put.summary: Replace the speaker→PJ mapping for a session.
put.description:
Replace the mapping in one atomic write.

- 404 if the session does not belong to the current MJ.
- 409 wrong-mode if the session is non_diarised (use /players instead).
- 409 if the session is not yet in ``state=transcribed``.
- 422 if any ``pj_id`` is unknown or owned by another MJ.

Side-effect: any existing ``pov:*`` artefact for this session is
deleted (data-model.md §6 invariant). A subsequent
``POST /artifacts/povs`` is required to regenerate them.

get.summary: Read the current speaker→PJ mapping for a session.
get.description:
Returns 200 with an empty dict when the session has no mapping
yet (resource exists but is not configured).

409 wrong-mode if the session is non_diarised (use GET /players).

post.summary: Replace the list of PJ present at a non_diarised session.
post.description:
Declare the PJ present at a non_diarised session (FR-012).

PUT-like semantics — the provided list replaces the previous one
integrally. Each ``pj_id`` must belong to the current MJ (422
``invalid-player-list`` otherwise). Reserved for non_diarised
sessions (409 ``wrong-mode`` on diarised).

get.summary: Read the current PJ list of a non_diarised session.
Fetch the diarised transcription of a session.
Export the transcription as Markdown (text/markdown).

post.summary: Trigger the narrative summary generation for a session.

get.summary: Fetch the narrative summary of a session.
Export the narrative summary as Markdown (text/markdown).

post.summary: Trigger the structured-elements card generation for a session.

get.summary: Fetch the structured-elements card of a session.
Export the structured-elements card as Markdown (text/markdown).

post.summary: Trigger the global session summary (map-reduce LLM).
post.description:
Enqueue the map-reduce summary job for a non_diarised session.

Pre-conditions:
- session must belong to the MJ (404 otherwise)
- session must be in non_diarised mode (409 wrong-mode)
- session must be in state=transcribed (409 session-not-transcribed)
- session must have at least 1 chunk (409 no-chunks)

Side effect documented under FR-011: each new summary job resets
chunks.summary_text and cascade-deletes existing narrative /
elements / pov:* artefacts for the session. The atomicity is
enforced by ``_generate_summary`` (see research.md §2).

get.summary: Fetch the global session summary (JSON).
Export the global session summary as Markdown (text/markdown).

post.summary: Trigger POV generation — one artefact per mapped PJ.
post.description:
Enqueue a single job that generates one ``pov:<pj_id>`` artefact
per row in the session's mapping. Pre-conditions match the other
generators (404 if foreign, 409 if not transcribed) plus FR-011:
a 409 ``no-mapping`` is raised when the session has no configured
speaker-PJ mapping yet — the operator must call
``PUT /mapping`` first.

get.summary: Fetch one PJ's POV — JSON, or Markdown via the .md suffix.
get.description:
One route handles both representations because the ``.md`` suffix
pattern would otherwise collide with the UUID path param at the
Starlette routing layer (``{pj_id}`` matches ``[^/]+`` which greedily
swallows the ``.md``).

- ``GET .../povs/<uuid>``     → JSON (:class:`PovArtifactOut`)
- ``GET .../povs/<uuid>.md``  → Markdown (``text/markdown``)

post.summary: Enroll a player and return the plaintext Bearer token (once).
post.description:
Create a player key bound to one of the GM's PJs.

The plaintext token is returned exactly once — store it now, the
server only keeps the Argon2 hash.

delete.summary: Revoke a player key (immediate effect on the next request).

get.summary: Profile of the current player (name + their PJ).
List the sessions where the current player's PJ is mapped.
Read the global narrative summary of a session (player view).
Export the global narrative summary as Markdown (player view).
Read the current player's POV summary for a session.
Export the current player's POV as Markdown.
Fetch the status of a JDR-service async job.
get.description:
Project an RQ job into the project's JobOut shape.

RQ holds the live state for 24h on success / 7d on failure. Cross-
tenant access (a GM polling another GM's job) returns 404 so the
endpoint never confirms the existence of a foreign job.

get.summary: Liveness alias (legacy Jalon 0).
get.description:
Legacy liveness endpoint kept for backward compatibility.

Equivalent to ``/healthz``. New monitoring setups should target
``/healthz`` (liveness) and ``/readyz`` (readiness) instead.

get.summary: Liveness probe (Jalon 6).
get.description:
Always 200 as long as the Python process is alive.

Does NOT check external dependencies. Use ``/readyz`` to decide
whether to send traffic. Intent: orchestrator should restart the
process iff this fails.

get.summary: Readiness probe — DB + Redis (Jalon 6).
get.description:
200 if every backing dependency is reachable, 503 otherwise.

Per-check status surfaced in the body so an operator can see
which dependency is down without parsing logs.

path.title: Path
path.description: Path on disk relative to KAEYRIS_DATA_DIR.

duration_seconds.title: Duration Seconds
duration_seconds.description: Audio duration in seconds. ``null`` when ``ffprobe`` is unavailable on the host — non-fatal.

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: RQ job identifier for the queued transcription.

audio.title: Audio
audio.description: Audio file (M4A). The upload itself is streamed to disk and has no hard size limit at this layer. Note: with TRANSCRIPTION_PROVIDER=cloud, the OpenAI Whisper API caps individual requests at 25 MB, so the transcription job has to chunk larger files (R3 — to be implemented). With provider=local (LAN GPU host running faster-whisper), any size is supported.

ChunkOut.title: ChunkOut
ChunkOut.description:
One row of `jdr_chunks` as exposed by `GET /sessions/{id}/chunks`.

`summary_text` is intentionally NOT exposed — it is internal to the
LLM pipeline (research.md §5).

Element.title: Element
Element.description:
One row of the four-category elements card.

The LLM may return arbitrary keys; we surface only ``name`` and
``description`` so the public contract is stable across model swaps.

ElementsArtifactOut.title: ElementsArtifactOut
ElementsArtifactOut.description:
Public projection of an ``Artifact(kind='elements')`` row.

The four lists are *always* present, even when empty (``[]``). See
acceptance scenario US 2.3 in ``spec.md``.

id.title: Id
id.description: RQ job identifier (echoed from the queue).

JobQueuedOut.title: JobQueuedOut
JobQueuedOut.description:
Reply to an artefact-trigger POST: a freshly enqueued RQ job.

Status starts at ``queued``; the worker updates it as it processes.
The full lifecycle (running -> succeeded/failed) is observable via
GET /jobs/{id} (added in sub-lot 3f).

title.title: Title
title.description: Human-readable title for the live session.

campaign_context.title: Campaign Context
campaign_context.description: Optional campaign-bible block (same semantics as batch mode).

LiveSessionInit.title: LiveSessionInit
LiveSessionInit.description:
Body that the future ``POST /live/sessions`` will accept.

Published as a Pydantic model so the OpenAPI schema is complete even
though the handler always raises 501.

MappingOut.title: MappingOut
MappingOut.description:
Response for both PUT and GET ``/mapping``.

``updated_at`` is the most recent ``updated_at`` across the rows,
or ``None`` when the session has no mapping yet.

text.title: Text
text.description: French narrative summary produced by the LLM.

token.title: Token
token.description: Plaintext Bearer token. Shown only on this 201 response. Store it now — the server only keeps an Argon2 hash.

PlayerOut.title: PlayerOut
PlayerOut.description:
Response of ``POST /services/jdr/players``.

``token`` is the *plaintext* Bearer token; it is returned **once** at
creation and never again. The server only stores the Argon2 hash.

text.title: Text
text.description: French POV summary scoped to this PJ.

PovArtifactOut.title: PovArtifactOut
PovArtifactOut.description:
Public projection of an ``Artifact(kind='pov:<pj_id>')`` row.

``pj_id`` is exposed at the top level for clients that don't want to
parse the composite ``kind`` field.

recorded_at.title: Recorded At
recorded_at.description: When the session actually took place (not the upload time). ISO-8601 with timezone.

transcription_mode.description: Optional. 'diarised' (default, Jalon 5 behaviour) or 'non_diarised' (chunked transcription + map-reduce LLM summary). Immutable after creation. Default applied server-side if omitted.

campaign_context.title: Campaign Context
campaign_context.description: Optional campaign-bible block prepended to every narrative / elements LLM prompt for this session. Use it to anchor the model on recurring PNJ, the campaign tone, or the current story arc. Can be updated later via PATCH.

SessionPlayersIn.title: SessionPlayersIn
SessionPlayersIn.description:
Body of `POST /services/jdr/sessions/{session_id}/players`.

Replaces the player list integrally (PUT-like semantics).

SessionState.title: SessionState
SessionState.description: Lifecycle of a session — see data-model.md §3.

SessionUpdate.title: SessionUpdate
SessionUpdate.description:
Payload accepted by ``PATCH /services/jdr/sessions/{id}``.

Every field is optional — the route applies only the keys that are
present (PATCH semantics). To clear ``campaign_context``, send
``"campaign_context": null`` explicitly (the route distinguishes
"unset" from "explicit null" via Pydantic's ``model_fields_set``).

TranscriptionMode.title: TranscriptionMode
TranscriptionMode.description:
Per-session transcription posture — see feature 002 data-model.md §2.

- ``DIARISED`` (default): Jalon 5 pipeline, segments stored in
  ``jdr_transcriptions`` with speaker labels.
- ``NON_DIARISED``: chunked transcription stored in ``jdr_chunks``,
  no speaker labels, downstream artefacts consume per-chunk summaries.

speaker_label.title: Speaker Label
speaker_label.description: Raw label produced by the backend ('speaker_1', 'unknown', …). The mapping to PJs is a per-session business decision exposed via /mapping (US3).

TranscriptionSegmentOut.title: TranscriptionSegmentOut
TranscriptionSegmentOut.description: One diarised utterance in a transcription.

kaeyris-bearer.description: Bearer token issued by the operator. Use `python scripts/generate_api_key.py <name>` to create one.
